item.title: About
item.description:
This is a simpler version of my CV. You can reach me on linked in at: https://www.linkedin.com/in/terrydai/

Summary:

  • I am a Software Engineer with many years of experience across a wide range of technology stacks and platforms.
  • Recent experience includes: building platform services, data products and pipelines, and leveraging AI tools to improve team productivity.
  • I am passionate about learning and knowledge sharing.

Objectives

  • To continuously improve my knowledge and practices in software design and engineering.
  • To collaborate closely with stakeholders to regularly and reliably deliver value to customers.
  • To work on impactful products / projects, preferably leveraging data and AI.
  • Mentoring and coaching: I love to share my knowledge and learning with my team.

Programming Languages

  • Scala
  • Java
  • TypeScript / JavaScript
  • C#
  • English

Skills & Technologies

  • MySQL / PostgreSQL
  • AWS (DynamoDB, RDS, RedShift, S3 etc)
  • Google Cloud (BigQuery)
  • Databricks
  • ElasticSearch
  • JSON / XML / REST API
  • Protobuf / gRPC API
  • Apache Kafka / Avro
  • Splunk
  • Functional Programming
  • Object Oriented Design and Development
  • Kubernetes
  • Git
  • Docker

Previously Worked At

  • Atlassian
  • Zendesk
  • REA Group (realestate.com.au)

…

item.title: Learning Notes - Getting Started
item.description:
I recently spent about 2hrs to learn rust-lang by following this guide - The Rust Programming Language.

I wrote down some notes and thoughts about the experiences.

This is the first part.

Install Rust on macOS X

$ curl –proto ‘=https’ –tlsv1.2 https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh

The installation process was quite nice. Everything was quite smooth and fast ⚡️. Not quite sure if it was because I chose 1) which is the default installation.
